steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
The left side ends with an incomplete independent clause: “The Australian Desert in Australasia is an arid [____]” (the verb is needs a noun complement).

The right side contains an independent clause preceded by a prepositional phrase: “with a total area of about 2,700,000 km 2, it is one of the largest deserts of any type” (subject it, verb is).

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content:
To join such a structure, a comma + coordinating conjunction is required. “desert, and” provides the missing noun and the needed comma + and. Options A and D omit the conjunction altogether; option C creates ungrammatical coordination (“desert and with”).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
